Systems convenors across the UK

Offline draft map for review. It opens locally, needs no web hosting, and uses no external map tiles.
87 mapped entries 78 unique places
Where several organisations share the same place, markers are offset slightly so they can be selected individually.

Legend

Local backboneSpecialist organisations (often national) with deep coordination expertise, apply evidence-based interventions, create a strong impact narrative and often have significant fundraising power.
Community anchorDeeply rooted local organisations growing into the full backbone role. Long term commitment to the place and existing relationships are a key strength.
Local and regional/national backboneLocal anchors with dual reporting to specialist regional or national organisations. Particularly relevant where scaling an approach across several places.
Public sector backboneLocal authority or combined authority as trusted catalyst. Often where civil society lack the capacity or experience in the short term, but can lead to power imbalances.
Partnership modelBackbone function distributed across multiple organisations and where no single organisation is in the lead. Works when no single anchor is able to lead, but adds complexity around accountability and governance.
Popup fields

Corra

Kirkcaldy · Scotland
Local and regional/national backbone Community infrastructure & civic systems
What they do
Independent Scottish grant-making foundation and participatory community development organisation · Kirkcaldy, Fife
Primary mechanism for change
Community power
Role in systemic change
Enabler, Advocate
Summary
Independent Scottish grant-making foundation and participatory community development organisation · Kirkcaldy, Fife Corra Foundation's 2020 ten-year strategy explicitly centres power-shifting, racial equity, and community voice as the foundations of social change — the ambition is that by 2030, power to create change rests with people themselves.
